About
Talk to an engineer

Electrical Signature Analysis

Every drivetrain fault leaves a trace in your electrical data.

SAM4 reads that trace from the motor control cabinet. It detects bearing wear, cavitation, misalignment, and winding defects weeks to months before failure. No sensors on the machine.

Request a Demo See How ESA Works

The cost of what you can't see

30-40% of critical rotating assets have no condition monitoring
£390K potential fines avoided from a single pump failure detection (Yorkshire Water)
70,000 manual inspection trips replaced per year (Yorkshire Water)
€50K+ saved per avoided unplanned shutdown (ArcelorMittal)

Traditional condition monitoring forces a trade-off: expensive sensor installations or no monitoring at all. For submerged pumps, enclosed drives, and hazardous-zone motors, most teams choose nothing. The result is failures that no one saw coming.

Understand the monitoring blind spot →

Monitor from the electrical cabinet. Not the machine.

SAM4 captures current and voltage signatures at the motor control cabinet. Those signatures reveal the condition of the entire drivetrain: motor, transmission, coupling, and driven load. Changes in spectral patterns identify developing faults weeks to months before failure.

Once connected, Samotics manages the full pipeline: signal capture, AI detection, engineer validation, and confirmed maintenance alerts.

No sensors on the machine

Monitors from the MCC. Submerged pumps, sealed compressors, ATEX zones, remote sites. All reachable.

See why this matters →

Install in under 30 minutes

Non-intrusive current clamps in the MCC. No shutdown, no scaffolding, no ATEX permits.

See how it installs →

Over 90% fault detection rate

Bearing wear, cavitation, misalignment, winding defects. Detected weeks to months before failure. Measured across 1,135 fault events over 12 months.

See the results →
How ESA works →
